key_insight: A large autoregressive generative model can directly design catalyst structures while conditioning on both categorical and continuous target properties, enabling controllable inverse design rather than brute-force screening.
engineering_relevance: This approach can accelerate catalyst discovery workflows by improving screening efficiency and producing candidate structures with desired adsorbates, compositions, and binding energies.
method: Pretrained a GPT-style model on 133 million catalyst structures.
Fine-tuned on ~460,000 optimized structures with categorical labels and binding energies for conditional generation.
Used a numerical embedding layer to handle mixed categorical and continuous conditioning in one autoregressive framework.
evidence_signal: The model reached 98% structural validity, 95% optimization validity, 93% joint match rate for adsorbate type and composition, and about 20% binding-energy match rate, which is a four-fold improvement over the training distribution.
watch_item: Performance on continuous property control is still limited, so practical deployment will need careful validation to ensure generated catalysts remain physically realistic and experimentally synthesizable.
paper_title: Toward Controllable Catalyst Inverse Design via Large-Scale Autoregressive Pretraining
